_comment: Image Compress Guide — English language pack
_usage: Pass this object as e.detail.lang in the 'init' CustomEvent dispatched to the iframe.
txt-header-sub: A guide to the feature that automatically compresses images to reduce file size at the point of upload.
txt-desc-body: When the image compression option is enabled, images are automatically compressed in the browser at the moment the user uploads a file. The compressed file is then sent to the server, resulting in faster uploads and reduced storage usage.
txt-note-off-body: The original image file is sent to the server without any compression. Upload speed may be slower for large files.

txt-note-on-body.html: The image is compressed in the browser before being sent to the server. Image quality is controlled by the Quality setting below.

txt-flow-warn.html: Unsupported formats (GIF, BMP, SVG, etc.) are sent to the server as-is without compression.

txt-format-desc: Compression applies only to the following three file formats. All other files are uploaded without compression.
txt-placeholder-desc: Insert a screenshot here showing the original and compressed file sizes after enabling the option.
txt-example-note: The figures above are for illustration only. Actual compression results may vary depending on image type, resolution, and quality setting.

txt-step1-body.html: Turn the image compression option ON in the settings panel below.

txt-step2-body.html: Set the quality value to a number between 0.01 and 1. Lower values produce smaller files; higher values preserve more of the original quality.

txt-step3-body: When you upload an image file, the browser automatically compresses it. The compressed file is saved to the server without any additional steps.
txt-step4-body: After the upload is complete, you can view both the original and compressed file sizes on screen.
txt-opt-compress-desc: Automatically compresses images on file upload.
txt-opt-width-desc: Limits the maximum width in pixels of uploaded images.
txt-opt-height-desc: Limits the maximum height in pixels of uploaded images.
